reviews.review_description: Played on the Oculus Quest 2, it delivers a solid performance. As a fellow VR gunslinger, I found the gameplay a satisfying challenge, and I enjoyed experimenting with the diverse weapon options. While I typically gravitate toward higher-fidelity VR titles like Population: One and Ghosts of Tabor, I was pleasantly surprised by the immersive experience--enough so to work up a sweat. I believe the game would benefit from expanded content, including additional boss encounters, diverse level designs, and a robust achievement system. This game shows promise, and for a wave-based shooter, it has successfully piqued my interest. Continued development will be key to realizing its full potential. Thanks for the gameplay!

developer_response.body:
Thanks for your comment. 
Your feedback was very accurate. 
We are evolving this same game into 4 new free sessions, including multiplayer mode, new stages, new bosses.
Regarding the achievement system, we are studying how to build it in a fun way that makes sense.
Your feedback was very important to our development team. Thanks!

reviews.review_description: Dynamic, fun and easy to learn. Love the the fact I can sit or stand and no vertigo motion sickness.

I found the game a lot of fun, laughing at the jokes and getting better as the levels went by. 
I loved the Frenzied Horde mode!
I liked the gameplay and graphics. I was surprised by the price.
